    <h1 {...{
      "id": "listening-to-inbox-events"
    }}>{`Listening to Inbox Events`}</h1>
    <p>{`This tutorial explains how to listen to events from the Inbox in your application.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`The Messaging extension provides a way to listen to events from both the Inbox container and individual content cards within it. By conforming to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxEventListening`}</inlineCode>{` protocol, you can respond to inbox-level state changes and individual content card interactions.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "inbox-level-events"
    }}>{`Inbox-Level Events`}</h2>
    <p>{`These events track the overall state of the inbox container.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "onloading"
    }}>{`onLoading`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when the inbox begins loading content. This happens when the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` is first created or when the user triggers a refresh (pull-to-refresh or programmatic).`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onLoading(_ inbox: InboxUI) {
    print("Inbox is loading...")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "onsuccess"
    }}>{`onSuccess`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when the inbox successfully loads content. This event is triggered whether the inbox contains cards or is empty.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onSuccess(_ inbox: InboxUI) {
    print("Inbox loaded successfully")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "onerror"
    }}>{`onError`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when the inbox encounters an error while loading. Common error scenarios include container settings not found, network failures, or invalid surface configuration.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onError(_ inbox: InboxUI, _ error: Error) {
    print("Inbox error: \\(error.localizedDescription)")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "content-card-events"
    }}>{`Content Card Events`}</h2>
    <p>{`These events track interactions with individual content cards within the inbox.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "oncardcreated"
    }}>{`onCardCreated`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when a content card is created and configured. This happens once per card when the inbox loads or refreshes.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardCreated(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
    print("Card created: \\(card.id)")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "oncarddisplayed"
    }}>{`onCardDisplayed`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when a content card is displayed to the user. Use this event to track impressions.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardDisplayed(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
    print("Card displayed: \\(card.id)")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote">{`Note - This event is triggered automatically by the SDK when the card appears on screen. You do not need to manually call any display methods.`}</p>
    </blockquote>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "oncardinteracted"
    }}>{`onCardInteracted`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when a user interacts with a content card (taps a button, clicks a link, etc.). The return value determines how the SDK handles the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`actionURL`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Parameters:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`card`}</inlineCode>{` - The content card that was interacted with`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`interactionId`}</inlineCode>{` - Unique identifier for the interaction (button ID, link ID, etc.)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`actionURL`}</inlineCode>{` - Optional URL associated with the interaction`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Return Value:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Return `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`true`}</inlineCode>{` if your application handled the URL (prevents SDK from opening it)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Return `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`false`}</inlineCode>{` to let the SDK open the URL`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardInteracted(_ card: ContentCardUI, _ interactionId: String, actionURL: URL?) -> Bool {
    guard let url = actionURL else { return false }
    
    if url.scheme == "myapp" {
        navigateToDeepLink(url)
        return true // URL handled by app
    }
    
    return false // Let SDK handle URL
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Read Status Tracking:`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`When a card is interacted with, it is automatically marked as `}<strong parentName="p">{`read`}</strong>{` and persisted. The read status persists between app launches and affects unread indicator visibility (if enabled).`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "oncarddismissed"
    }}>{`onCardDismissed`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when a user dismisses a content card by tapping the dismiss button.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardDismissed(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
    print("Card dismissed: \\(card.id)")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote">{`Note - The Inbox automatically removes dismissed cards from the UI.`}</p>
    </blockquote>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "implement-inboxeventlistening"
    }}>{`Implement InboxEventListening`}</h2>
    <p>{`To listen to inbox events, conform to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxEventListening`}</inlineCode>{` protocol and pass the listener to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`getInboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` API.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`import SwiftUI
import AEPMessaging

struct InboxPage: View, InboxEventListening {
    
    let inboxUI: InboxUI
    @State private var showError = false
    @State private var errorMessage = ""
    
    init() {
        let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")
        inboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: inboxSurface, listener: self)
        inboxUI.isPullToRefreshEnabled = true
    }
    
    var body: some View {
        inboxUI.view
            .onAppear {
                Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces([Surface(path: "inbox")])
            }
    }
    
    // MARK: - Inbox State Events
    
    func onLoading(_ inbox: InboxUI) {
        print("Loading inbox...")
    }
    
    func onSuccess(_ inbox: InboxUI) {
        print("Inbox loaded successfully")
    }
    
    func onError(_ inbox: InboxUI, _ error: Error) {
        print("Inbox error: \\(error)")
        errorMessage = error.localizedDescription
        showError = true
    }
    
    // MARK: - Content Card Events
    
    func onCardCreated(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
        print("Card created: \\(card.id)")
    }
    
    func onCardDisplayed(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
        print("Card displayed: \\(card.id)")
    }
    
    func onCardInteracted(_ card: ContentCardUI, _ interactionId: String, actionURL: URL?) -> Bool {
        AnalyticsService.trackInteraction(cardId: card.id, interactionId: interactionId)
        
        if let url = actionURL, url.scheme == "myapp" {
            handleDeepLink(url)
            return true
        }
        
        return false
    }
    
    func onCardDismissed(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
        print("Card dismissed: \\(card.id)")
    }
    
    private func handleDeepLink(_ url: URL) {
        // Your deep link handling logic
        print("Handling deep link: \\(url)")
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "handling-actionable-urls"
    }}>{`Handling Actionable URLs`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`onCardInteracted`}</inlineCode>{` method provides control over how URLs are handled when users interact with content cards. Here are common patterns:`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "pattern-1-handle-all-urls"
    }}>{`Pattern 1: Handle All URLs`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardInteracted(_ card: ContentCardUI, _ interactionId: String, actionURL: URL?) -> Bool {
    guard let url = actionURL else { return false }
    
    // Handle all URLs in your app
    navigateToURL(url)
    return true // Prevent SDK from opening URL
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "pattern-2-selective-handling"
    }}>{`Pattern 2: Selective Handling`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardInteracted(_ card: ContentCardUI, _ interactionId: String, actionURL: URL?) -> Bool {
    guard let url = actionURL else { return false }
    
    // Only handle specific URL schemes
    if url.scheme == "myapp" || url.scheme == "http" || url.scheme == "https" {
        handleURL(url)
        return true
    }
    
    // Let SDK handle other schemes
    return false
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "best-practices"
    }}>{`Best Practices`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Avoid Heavy Work in Event Handlers`}</strong>{`: Event handlers are called synchronously on the main thread. Keep event handlers lightweight and dispatch heavy work to background queues.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Handle Errors Gracefully`}</strong>{`: Provide user-friendly error messages and retry options:`}</p>
        <pre parentName="li"><code parentName="pre" {...{
            "className": "language-swift"
          }}>{`func onError(_ inbox: InboxUI, _ error: Error) {
    showAlert(title: "Unable to load inbox", message: "Please try again later.")
}
`}</code></pre>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Log for Debugging`}</strong>{`: Use event handlers to log state transitions during development:`}</p>

            "className": "language-swift"
          }}>{`func onSuccess(_ inbox: InboxUI) {

    #endif
}
`}</code></pre>
      </li>
    </ol>
